chief_complaint: A 66-year-old Mexican male patient presented with intermittent chest pain, shortness of breath, and neurological symptoms.
history: The patient has a family history significant for hereditary amyloidosis. He presented with intermittent chest pain, shortness of breath, and neurological symptoms such as numbness.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: An echocardiogram showed an ejection fraction of 55-60% with grade 2 diastolic dysfunction. A technetium pyrophosphate scan was used to make the diagnosis of transthyretin amyloidosis, and genetic testing showed a p.G67A pathogenic mutation in the transthyretin (TTR) gene.
